structure.summary: In columns 0-23 this is NOT a dungeon-prop sheet. It is an RPG-Maker BUILDING sheet (flat-roof blob, gabled shingle roof, two-material floor blob, 2-tile-tall wall elevation strip with doorway/stairs/altar/collapse pieces) repeated THREE TIMES in three palettes. Verified by a per-cell alpha scan: rows 1-10, 16-25 and 35-44 have byte-identical opacity masks.

variants.name: red terracotta overgrown ruin (Aztec/Maya meander)
variants.accent: greek-key/meander relief + gold stripe, hanging vines

structure.emptyRows: rows 0, 8, 11-15, 26-34, 45-51 are fully transparent across c0-23
structure.voidColor: #362e3f flat dark purple. It is OPAQUE, not transparent. It is the artist's 'interior/void' fill behind doorways, under roofs and inside collapsed walls. Do NOT mistake it for alpha.

blockCandidates.name: pale_ruin_stone
blockCandidates.why: Pale sage-grey masonry with fine diagonal crack hatching; fully opaque, uniform, no border. Identical variants at c16-c22 r2. The best generic 'ruined temple / catacomb' wall we lack.

blockCandidates.name: crypt_slate_tile
blockCandidates.why: Blue-grey square floor tiles with dark grout and an occasional darker slab; fully opaque, reads as a cold crypt/ossuary floor. Variants (15,3)(16,3)(18,3)(19,3).

blockCandidates.name: crypt_slate_tile_center
blockCandidates.why: AUTOTILE-BLOB CENTRE. The blue-tile blob demo occupies c16-c18 x r4-r6 with an 8px-offset dark rim; (17,5) is the ONLY interior cell with no rim on any edge. Safe fill if you want the bordered variant.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_arrow_up
blockCandidates.why: Framed pale-stone floor plate engraved with an up arrow. Fully opaque, self-contained. Puzzle/pressure-plate mood for catacombs.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_arrow_right
blockCandidates.why: Same plate family, right arrow. Fully opaque.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_ring
blockCandidates.why: Pale plate with a chunky blue-grey ring/torus glyph. Fully opaque; eldritch seal mood.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_arrow_left
blockCandidates.why: Plate, left arrow. Fully opaque.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_arrow_down
blockCandidates.why: Plate, down arrow. Fully opaque.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_square
blockCandidates.why: Plate with a solid blue square inset. Fully opaque.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_raised
blockCandidates.why: Plate with a bright raised square boss (reads as a pushed/pressed button). Fully opaque.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_recessed
blockCandidates.why: Plate with a recessed square frame — the 'unpressed' twin of (20,6). Fully opaque.

blockCandidates.name: rune_plate_triangle
blockCandidates.why: Plate with an outlined triangle glyph. Fully opaque; completes the 3x3 plate set at c20-c22 x r4-r6.

blockCandidates.name: temple_wall_frieze
blockCandidates.why: TOP half of a 2-tall wall: cobalt dentil frieze band over cream ashlar. Fully opaque and horizontally tileable. PAIR ONLY — stack it above (5,10), never above itself.

blockCandidates.name: temple_wall_base
blockCandidates.why: BOTTOM half of the same wall: cream ashlar over a cobalt striped balustrade skirt. Fully opaque, horizontally tileable. There is NO pure-ashlar 16px cell anywhere in the strip — the frieze/skirt bands eat it.

blockCandidates.name: temple_shingle_blue
blockCandidates.why: Cream slat shingles with cobalt accents running one diagonal direction; fully opaque. CAVEAT: it is a hip-roof piece, so tiling it will show a repeating diagonal seam — good as a slanted roof face, mediocre as a flat block face.

blockCandidates.name: sandstone_brick
blockCandidates.why: Brown sandstone ashlar blocks with dark mortar; fully opaque, uniform. Variants c16-c22 r17. A warmer, chunkier brick than our existing dark_bricks.

blockCandidates.name: rippled_sand
blockCandidates.why: Bright gold sand with wind-ripple striations; fully opaque, uniform across (15,18)-(19,18). Nicer than a flat sand tile for Cinderrift/desert floors.

blockCandidates.name: sand_with_slab
blockCandidates.why: Rippled sand with a half-buried brown stone slab. Fully opaque; scatter tile for buried-ruin floors. Cousins (21,18) two small slabs, (22,18) one wide slab.

blockCandidates.name: gold_temple_wall_top
blockCandidates.why: TOP half of the Egyptian wall: gold cornice bands over a plain recessed gold panel. Fully opaque; pair with (5,25).

blockCandidates.name: gold_temple_wall_base
blockCandidates.why: BOTTOM half: gold panel over cornice + pale sill band. Fully opaque; pair with (5,24).

blockCandidates.name: hieroglyph_wall
blockCandidates.why: Gold wall panel carved with figure hieroglyphs (standing figures / ankh-like signs). Fully opaque. Variants (11,24) and (12,24) with different figures; (8,24) is a two-line glyph tablet, (7,24) a zigzag glyph.

blockCandidates.name: ruin_cobble_red
blockCandidates.why: Rounded red-brown boulders/cobbles; fully opaque, uniform. Variants c16-c22 r36. Great 'ancient overgrown ruin' base stone.

blockCandidates.name: moss_carpet
blockCandidates.why: Dense bright-green leafy moss, fully opaque edge to edge across (15,37)-(19,37). Exactly the mossy-overgrown-ruin mood we lack.

blockCandidates.name: mossy_cobble
blockCandidates.why: Red cobble with a few small moss tufts growing on it — fully opaque, self-contained, the perfect transition block between ruin_cobble_red and moss_carpet. Variants (21,37) and (22,37) with different tuft placements.

blockCandidates.name: cobble_plate_triangle
blockCandidates.why: Terracotta version of the engraved plate set (triangle). Full 3x3 plate set lives at c20-c22 x r38-r40, matching the grey set at r4-r6.

blockCandidates.name: meander_frieze_terracotta
blockCandidates.why: Terracotta wall band carved with a greek-key/meander relief above a gold inlay stripe. Fully opaque, horizontally tileable. TOP half of a 2-tall wall; pair with (5,44).

blockCandidates.name: meander_relief_terracotta
blockCandidates.why: BOTTOM half: broad terracotta relief course over a second meander band. Fully opaque. Together with (5,43) this is the strongest 'ancient eldritch temple' wall on the sheet.

blockCandidates.name: shingle_gold
blockCandidates.why: Gold-palette twin of (6,6) at the +15 row offset — same diagonal slat shingle, same caveat about the chevron seam.

blockCandidates.name: shingle_terracotta
blockCandidates.why: Terracotta+gold-accent twin of (6,6) at the +34 row offset. (6,41) is the same shingle heavily overgrown with moss along its lower edge — a lovely 'collapsed roof' block.

blockCandidates.name: pale_stone_inlay_pair
blockCandidates.why: Pale ruin stone with two small blue slate insets. Fully opaque; decorative floor variation. Cousin (22,3) has one wide blue inset.

autotileRegions.kind: flat parapet roof / building footprint blob
autotileRegions.detail: A pale stone rim drawn around a field of the flat #362e3f void colour, laid out with 8px (half-tile) offsets. (2,2) is a tiny closed square rim; (2,3)-(4,5) is the large open rectangle. EVERY cell here is either a rim corner/edge or a featureless dark-purple interior. NOTHING in this rect is a usable standalone block.
autotileRegions.danger: high — the rim pieces look like plain wall in a thumbnail

autotileRegions.detail: A full pitched roof drawn as one picture. The ridge apex sits between c9 and c10 (row 3 has the peak notch), shingle chevrons converge on it from both sides, and r7 is where the roof meets the wall below (blue-tile wall visible under the eaves). Row 1 holds the transparent gable/eave-edge caps. Only the outermost columns (c6, c13) read as a plain one-direction shingle field.
autotileRegions.danger: high — grabbing c9/c10 gets you the ridge chevron

autotileRegions.detail: The variant's SECONDARY material (blue slate / gold sand / green moss) is painted as an irregular blob over its PRIMARY material (pale stone / sandstone brick / red cobble). Clean pure cells: r2 c16-c19 = pure primary; r3 c15-c19 = pure secondary. Everything in r4-r6 x c15-c19 straddles the blob boundary EXCEPT (17,5), which is blob interior. Note (15,2) / (15,17) / (15,36) are transparent.
autotileRegions.danger: medium — r4-r6 cells ship with a blob notch

autotileRegions.detail: A 2x2 wall panel with dark pilaster borders baked onto its LEFT (c1) and RIGHT (c2) edges. Never use as fill.

Strictly a top row (r9) + bottom row (r10) pair. Horizontally it is a repeating run of frieze/ashlar/balustrade with ornaments dropped in: (6,9)/(6,10) hanging lamp-box + pedestal, (7,9)/(7,10) framed plaques, (8,9) blue 'I' emblem tablet, (9,9)/(9,10) tall pilaster column with a blue vertical inlay, (10,9)-(12,9)/(10,10)-(12,10) a big blue-framed studded inset panel. Gold twin: (7,24) zigzag glyph, (8,24) glyph tablet, (9,24)/(9,25) a standing IDOL STATUE, (10,24)-(12,24) figure hieroglyphs. Red twin: (6,43)-(9,44) hanging vines, (9,44)-(12,44) recessed niche panel.
autotileRegions.danger: medium — ornament cells are not tileable; only (4,9)/(5,9)+(4,10)/(5,10) are plain

autotileRegions.detail: (14,*) wall with a right-hand column, (15,*) the dark doorway void under a lintel, (16,*) a left-hand column + wall, (17,*) a dark opening under a triangle frieze containing a descending stone STAIRCASE, (18,*) a dark void containing a stepped altar/pedestal with a shelf ledge. All opaque (the openings are painted #362e3f, not alpha).

(20,*) wall with a jagged crack down its right edge, (21,*) shattered wall fragment, (22,*) wall with a jagged crack down its left edge, (23,*) SOLID FLAT #362e3f void (no texture at all). These are a scenic composite, not blocks.

Called out so nobody skips them. r3 = inlay floor variants; r4-r6 = a 3x3 set of engraved floor plates (arrow up/right/ring, arrow left/down/square, raised boss/recessed frame/triangle). All fully opaque and independently usable.

decorations.verdict: Columns 0-23 contain almost NO transparent prop decorations. A per-cell alpha scan (256 px/cell) found every cell in this half is either 100% opaque or 100% transparent, with exactly four partial cells per palette variant — and those four are roof edges, not props. There are no chains, bars, skulls, urns or braziers in this column half; if they exist on this sheet they must live in c24-c48.

partiallyTransparentCells.what: Roof EAVE caps: dark-purple roof body cut by a pale diagonal edge running down-right at (8,1) and down-left at (9,1) — the two halves of a shallow V. ~50-90% opaque.
Roof GABLE caps: pale-and-blue shingle slopes rising to the right at (11,1) and to the left at (12,1), transparent above the slope line.
Gold-palette twins of the four above (identical alpha mask; (11,16)/(12,16) are thinner, <50% opaque).
Terracotta-palette twins of the four above (I looked: (11,35)/(12,35) are terracotta slopes with a gold accent line).

opaqueDetailWorthPrefabbing.what: A standing IDOL STATUE carved into the gold wall — rounded head, shoulders, and a plinth that runs into the base course. Best used as a prefab wall inlay, not a block.
Pale PILASTER COLUMN with a full-height cobalt inlay strip. Terracotta twin at (9,43)/(9,44) has gold caps top and bottom.
Hanging blue LAMP/SCONCE box under the frieze with a matching pedestal below.
Cobalt 'I' / hourglass EMBLEM tablet set into the ashlar.
Descending stone STAIRCASE inside a dark opening (terracotta version at (17,43)/(17,44), gold at (17,24)/(17,25)).
Stepped stone ALTAR / pedestal in a dark void with a shelf ledge above — a ready-made shrine centrepiece.

Green VINES trailing down the terracotta meander wall. IMPORTANT: they are painted directly onto the opaque wall, NOT drawn on alpha — you cannot lift them out as a cross-quad without repainting the backdrop.
A single moss tuft on red cobble (opaque background).
Jagged wall COLLAPSE / crack pieces. Opaque, with #362e3f void showing through the breach — usable as prefab detail if the block behind is also dark.

notes: DISCIPLINE: every coordinate here was read off a magnified, gridded render of the source PNG (tools/out/zoom/*, regenerated in-session) plus the labelled contact sheets. Where I could not resolve a cell I said so rather than guessing.

The three palette variants are the same layout at row offsets 0 / +15 / +34. Verified by a per-cell alpha mask that is byte-identical for rows 1-10, 16-25, 35-44. So any coordinate (c,r) in the grey set has a gold twin at (c,r+15) and a terracotta twin at (c,r+34).

#362e3f (flat dark purple) is OPAQUE. It is the sheet's painted 'void'. If you alpha-key a tile against transparency you will still ship a purple hole. Any tile touching a doorway, roof underside or wall breach carries it.

There is no pure-ashlar 16x16 cell in the wall elevation strips: the frieze band (top ~5px of r9) and the balustrade skirt (bottom ~6px of r10) always intrude. Wall blocks from this sheet must be built as a 2-block vertical pair, or the bands must be repainted in-pipeline (we already paint web/ash/charred_log, so this is precedent-following).

Moods this half actually delivers, ranked: (1) mossy overgrown ruin — moss_carpet + mossy_cobble + ruin_cobble_red + vine wall, (2) ancient/eldritch temple — terracotta meander relief + engraved rune plates + stepped altar, (3) crypt/catacomb — pale_ruin_stone + crypt_slate_tile + cobalt frieze wall. It delivers NO sewer, NO prison bars, NO ossuary bone.
Rows 0, 8, 11-15, 26-34 and 45-51 are completely empty across c0-23 — do not waste an extraction pass on them.
(23,9) / (23,24) / (23,43) are a single flat untextured #362e3f square. Technically a fully-opaque tile, but featureless; if a pitch-black block is wanted, author it, don't extract it.
